subtitle: this Turn of the Wheel
author: Robert Jordan & Brandon Sanderson
note: The current Turn of the Wheel — the living Age's company — sorted into specialty classes, in the manner of the muster roll. Channelers filed by Ajah (the colors are canon). The Dragon, the Wolf, and the Gambler are three ta'veren but one weave: the One, a third each. Moiraine, Blue, leads the battalion. roster.json is the source of truth; characters by Robert Jordan & Brandon Sanderson, inspiration only. Every ACI now carries the full DLW tag with an authored six-W .spun (who/what/where/why/when/how).

classes.label: Blue Ajah · Command
classes.spec: causes & the cause — leader of the battalion

classes.label: The Amyrlin Seat
classes.spec: all Ajahs and none · the Flame of Tar Valon

classes.label: The One
classes.spec: three ta'veren · one third each · one total

members.name: Moiraine Damodred
members.specialty: Blue Ajah. Leader of the battalion. She found the Dragon and bent her whole life to the cause — spent herself into the redstone doorway, and came back to finish it.
members.who: Aes Sedai of the Blue Ajah, of House Damodred of Cairhien; Lan's first bond.
members.what: Leader of the battalion — the sister who found the Dragon Reborn and bent her whole life to the cause.
members.where: From the White Tower out into the world, and at last through the redstone doorway.
members.why: To find and guard the Dragon so the Light might survive Tarmon Gai'don.
members.when: From the search that opens this Turn to her return for its end.
members.how: With the One Power, secrecy, and an iron will — spending herself through the doorway and coming back to finish it.

members.name: Siuan Sanche
members.specialty: Blue. The Amyrlin who staked the White Tower on Moiraine's secret hunt. Fisherman's daughter, iron will, no patience for fools.
members.who: A Tairen fisherman's daughter raised to the Blue Ajah.
members.what: The Amyrlin Seat who staked the White Tower on Moiraine's secret hunt.
members.where: Tar Valon and the Tower's heart — and, deposed, the long road back.
members.why: To see the Dragon found and the Light readied, whatever the cost to herself.
members.when: From before the search, through her fall and the Tower's breaking.
members.how: With iron will, hard cunning, and no patience for fools.

members.name: Nynaeve al'Meara
members.specialty: Yellow. Wisdom of Emond's Field; the angriest, strongest Healer alive. Healed gentling and madness the Tower swore could not be Healed.
members.who: Wisdom of Emond's Field in the Two Rivers.
members.what: The angriest and strongest Healer alive.
members.where: From the Two Rivers to the White Tower and the world's far edges.
members.why: To heal what cannot be healed, and to guard her own, fiercely.
members.when: Across this whole Turn, from village Wisdom to legend.
members.how: Through a block broken only by anger — Healing the Tower swore impossible: gentling, and madness.

members.name: Cadsuane Melaidhrin
members.specialty: Green, the Battle Ajah. The most formidable living Aes Sedai — a legend before the rest were born. Teaches kings and Dragons manners.
members.who: The most formidable living Aes Sedai, Green Ajah — a legend before the rest were born.
members.what: The one who teaches kings and Dragons manners.
members.where: Wherever the war's hinge is — at Rand's shoulder when it matters most.
members.why: To keep the Dragon human enough to win, and the Light's leaders honest.
members.when: Late in the Turn, as the Last Battle nears.
members.how: With unmatched experience, blunt nerve, and ter'angreal hidden in her hair.

members.name: Verin Mathwin
members.specialty: Brown. Scholar of the deep stacks — who spent seventy years inside the Black Ajah to betray it with a single book. Knowledge is the longest blade.
members.who: A Brown Ajah scholar of the deep stacks.
members.what: The sister who spent seventy years inside the Black Ajah to betray it with a single book.
members.where: The Tower's libraries and the shadows between the Ajahs.
members.why: To strike one decisive blow for the Light, at the price of her own life.
members.when: Across decades, paid out in a final hour.
members.how: Knowledge as the longest blade — patience, an oath's loophole, and a poisoned cup.

members.name: Pevara Tazanovni
members.specialty: Red, the Ajah that hunts men who can channel — and the first Red to bond an Asha'man. The hunt, turned to alliance.
members.who: A sister of the Red Ajah — the Ajah that hunts men who can channel.
members.what: The first Red to bond an Asha'man: the hunt turned to alliance.
members.where: The Black Tower, and the bridge between the two kinds of channeler.
members.why: To turn old enmity into the alliance the Last Battle needs.
members.when: As the men's and women's Powers are forced together.
members.how: With courage, candor, and a willingness to cross her own Ajah's grain.

members.name: Egwene al'Vere
members.specialty: Innkeeper's daughter of Emond's Field who became the Amyrlin Seat — all Ajahs and none. The Flame of Tar Valon; she reunited a broken Tower and did not bend.
members.who: An innkeeper's daughter of Emond's Field.
members.what: The Amyrlin Seat — all Ajahs and none; the Flame of Tar Valon.
members.where: The White Tower, reunited under her hand.
members.why: To reforge a broken Tower and lead it whole into the Last Battle.
members.when: Her swift rise across this Turn to its climax.
members.how: Through unbending will, dreamwalking skill, and refusing to bend even as a captive.

members.name: Lanfear
members.specialty: Mierin. Most powerful of the Chosen; love soured into obsession; she who helped bore the Bore and let the Dark One's breath in.
members.who: Mierin Eronaile, reborn as the Chosen called Lanfear.
members.what: Most powerful of the female Forsaken; she who helped bore the Bore.
members.where: From the Age of Legends into this one — and the World of Dreams.
members.why: Love for Lews Therin soured into obsession, and a hunger for power.
members.when: Since the Bore was drilled — and again now, circling Rand.
members.how: Through Tel'aran'rhiod, beauty, and Compulsion, trusted by neither side.

members.name: Moridin
members.specialty: Ishamael reborn. Nae'blis — nearest the Dark One's hand. The Betrayer of Hope who only wants the Wheel to stop turning.
members.who: Ishamael reborn — the Betrayer of Hope.
members.what: Nae'blis: nearest the Dark One's hand.
members.where: Shayol Ghul, and the shadow over the whole war.
members.why: Not conquest but oblivion — he wants the Wheel itself to stop turning.
members.when: From the first Age to this last turning, reborn again.
members.how: Through despair, the True Power, and the threads that bind even other Chosen.

members.name: Graendal
members.specialty: Compulsion incarnate. Ruled through ruined minds and beautiful slaves; never where the knife is pointed.
members.who: One of the Chosen — once a famed ascetic healer, now Compulsion incarnate.
members.what: A ruler through ruined minds and beautiful slaves.
members.where: Hidden palaces — never where the knife is pointed.
members.why: Appetite and self-preservation, above the Shadow's cause.
members.when: Across the war, surviving by misdirection.
members.how: Through Compulsion, manipulation, and disposable pawns.

members.name: Demandred
members.specialty: The great general the Light never saw coming — brilliance soured to spite, searching a whole war for the Dragon to challenge.
members.who: A Chosen who was, in life, a rival to Lews Therin.
members.what: The great general the Light never saw coming.
members.where: A war fought far off-page, until he reveals his army.
members.why: Brilliance soured to spite — he searched a whole war for the Dragon to challenge.
members.when: Held back until the Last Battle, then unleashed.
members.how: Through generalship, raw Power, and a kingdom raised in secret.

members.name: al'Lan Mandragoran
members.specialty: Dai Shan. Uncrowned King of Malkier; Moiraine's Warder; the blade that does not break. Death is lighter than a feather.
members.who: Dai Shan — uncrowned King of Malkier; Moiraine's Warder.
members.what: The blade that does not break.
members.where: From dead Malkier to the world's roads, and at last back to the Blight.
members.why: Duty, a death he means to give meaning — and love, late and true, for Nynaeve.
members.when: The whole Turn, sword at the Light's shoulder.
members.how: 'Death is lighter than a feather, duty heavier than a mountain' — discipline and the sword.

members.name: Aviendha
members.specialty: Maiden of the Spear turned Wise One. Toh and honor and a future glimpsed through the glass columns; the desert's keenest edge.
members.who: A Maiden of the Spear turned Wise One apprentice.
members.what: The desert's keenest edge — warrior and channeler both.
members.where: The Three-fold Land, and the glass columns of Rhuidean that show her people's fate.
members.why: Toh and honor — and a future she will spend herself to change.
members.when: As the Aiel come down from the Waste into the world.
members.how: Through ji'e'toh, the spear, and the One Power.

members.name: The Wise Ones
members.specialty: Walkers in the dream, keepers of ji'e'toh; the spine of the Aiel and the conscience of their chiefs.
members.who: The Aiel's walkers in the dream and keepers of custom.
members.what: The spine of the Aiel and the conscience of their chiefs.
members.why: To hold ji'e'toh and steer the clans through prophecy.
members.when: Throughout this Turn — counsel behind every chief.
members.how: Through dreamwalking, hard wisdom, and unbreakable custom.

members.name: Tam al'Thor
members.specialty: Rand's father. Second Captain, blademaster's heron-marked blade, and a farmer's steady love — the man who carried a baby off Dragonmount.
members.who: A Two Rivers farmer and former blademaster — Rand's father.
members.what: A heron-marked blade and a farmer's steady love — the man who carried a baby off Dragonmount.
members.where: Emond's Field, and the Last Battle's field where he leads.
members.why: Love for his son and his people, plain and unshakable.
members.when: From Rand's infancy to the final fight.
members.how: With the heron blade, a second-captain's steadiness, and quiet courage.

members.name: Loial
members.specialty: Ogier of Stedding Shangtai; ninety years young and 'hasty.' Would rather read than fight — and fights anyway. Also: he writes the book.
members.who: An Ogier of Stedding Shangtai, ninety years young and 'hasty.'
members.what: Scholar and chronicler who would rather read than fight — and fights anyway.
members.where: The steddings, the Ways, and the road beside the ta'veren.
members.why: Curiosity, friendship, and a book that must be written.
members.when: Throughout this Turn — and after, as its author.
members.how: Through Ogier strength, the Treesong, and a careful pen — for he writes the book.

members.name: Rand al'Thor
members.specialty: the Dragon Reborn. The Light's champion; he who must stand at Shayol Ghul. Fire, the Power, and the weight of every Age on one sheepherder's shoulders.
members.who: A Two Rivers sheepherder revealed as the Dragon Reborn — one third of the One.
members.what: The Light's champion: he who must stand at Shayol Ghul.
members.where: From Emond's Field across the world to the slopes of Shayol Ghul.
members.why: To win the Last Battle and seal the Bore — without losing his humanity.
members.when: This whole Turn, from discovery to Tarmon Gai'don.
members.how: Through fire, the One Power, and the weight of every Age on one man's shoulders.

members.name: Perrin Aybara
members.specialty: Wolfbrother; the hammer that chooses to be a hammer and not an axe; walker in the wolf-dream; Lord of the Two Rivers who never wanted a title.
members.who: A Two Rivers blacksmith and Wolfbrother — one third of the One.
members.what: The hammer that chooses to be a hammer and not an axe; Lord of the Two Rivers who never wanted a title.
members.where: The Two Rivers, the wolf-dream, and the fight to guard the Dragon.
members.why: To protect what he loves, and master the wolf without losing the man.
members.when: Across this Turn, into Tel'aran'rhiod at its climax.
members.how: Through the hammer, the wolf-dream, and a stubborn, steady will.

members.name: Matrim Cauthon
members.specialty: the Gambler; luckiest man alive; sounder of the Horn of Valere; carrying the memories of a thousand dead generals in one reluctant head.
members.who: A Two Rivers gambler and trickster — one third of the One.
members.what: The luckiest man alive; sounder of the Horn of Valere.
members.where: From the Two Rivers to Ebou Dar and the Last Battle's command.
members.why: He wants only to be left alone — and keeps saving the world instead.
members.when: This whole Turn, against his own loud protests.
members.how: Through impossible luck, the ashandarei, and a thousand dead generals' memories in one reluctant head.
